title: Solid state forms of (2e)-n-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide and process for preparation thereof
abstract: The Present invention relates to solid state forms of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(N-phenyl sulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide represented by the following structural formula-1 and process for the preparation thereof.
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1. Amorphous (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide. 2. Amorphous form of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide as claimed in claim- 1 is characterized by P-XRD pattern as depicted in figure- 1. 3. A process for the preparation of amorphous form of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3 -[3 -(phenyl sulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1, comprising of: a) Dissolving the (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 in a suitable solvent or mixture of solvents, b) optionally filtering the reaction mixture, c) removing the solvent from the reaction mixture to provide amorphous form of (2E)-N- hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1. 4. The process as claimed in claim-2, wherein, in step-a) the suitable solvent can be selected from chloro solvents, ketone solvents, C 1 -C 6 alcohol solvents, ester solvents, nitrile solvents, ether solvents or their mixtures; and the dissolution of compound of formula- 1 in a suitable solvent or mixture of solvents can be carried out at 25-30Â°C or by heating the reaction mixture to a temperature ranging from 30Â°C to reflux temperature of the solvents employed; in step-c) suitable techniques which may be used for the removal of solvent from the reaction mixture by evaporation, evaporation under reduced pressure, flash evaporation, vacuum drying, concentrating the reaction mixture, atmospheric distillation, vacuum distillation, distillation by using a rotational distillation device such as a Buchi Rotavapor, agitated thin film drying (ATFD), melt extrusion, spray drying, freeze drying (lyophilization), spray-freeze drying, addition of suitable anti-solvent to the reaction mixture followed by filtration of the precipitated solid, cooling the clear solution to lower temperatures such as below 20Â°C to precipitate the solid followed by filtration or by any other suitable techniques. 5. A process for the preparation of amorphous form of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenyl sulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1, comprising of: 

 a) Dissolving the (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 in methanol, b) fi Itering the reaction mi xture, c) distilling the solvent from the filtrate obtained in step-b) to provide amorphous form of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1. 6. A process for the preparation of amorphous form of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenyl sulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1, comprising of: a) Dissolving the (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 in methanol, b) filtering the reaction mixture, c) spray drying the filtrate obtained in step-b) to provide amorphous form of (2E)-N- hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula-1. 7. Amorphous solid dispersion of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2- enamide compound of formula-1 and at least pharmaceutically acceptable excipient. 8. Amorphous solid dispersion of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2- enamide compound of formula-1 and at least pharmaceutically acceptable excipient as claimed in claim-7, wherein, the excipient is selected from polyvinylpyrrolidone (povidone or PVP), polyvinylpolypyrrolidone, polysorbate, cross linked polyvinyl pyrrolidone (crospovidone), polyethylene glycol (macrogol or PEG), polyvinyl alcohol, polyvinyl chloride, polyvinyl acetate, propylene glycol, cellulose, cellulose acetate phthalate (CAP), methyl cellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC, its sodium and calcium salts), carboxymethylethyl cellulose (CMEC), ethyl cellulose, hydroxymethyl cellulose, ethyl hydroxyethyl cellulose, hydroxyethylcellulose, hydroxypropyl cellulose (HPC), hydroxypropyl cellulose acetate succinate (HPCAS), hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose (hypromellose or HPMC), hydroxypropyl methylcellulose acetate succinate (HPMC-AS), hydroxyethyl methyl cellulose succinate (HEMCS), hydroxypropyl methylcellulose phthalate (HPMC-P), hydroxypropyl methylcellulose acetate phthalate, microcrystalline 

 cellulose (MCC), cross linked sodium carboxymethyl cellulose (croscarmellose sodium), cross linked calcium carboxymethyl cellulose, magnesium stearate, aluminium stearate, calcium stearate, magnesium carbonate, talc, iron oxide (red, yellow, black), stearic acid, dextrates, dextrin, dextrose, sucrose, glucose, xylitol, lactitol, sorbitol, mannitol, maltitol, maltose, raffinose, fructose, maltodextrin, anhydrous lactose, lactose monohydrate, starches such as maize starch or corn starch, sodium starch glycolate, sodium carboxymethyl starch, pregelatinized starch, gelatin, sodium dodecyl sulfate, edetate disodium, sodium phosphate, sodium lauryl sulfate, triacetin, sucralose, calcium phosphate; polydextrose, Î±-, Î²-, Î³-cyclodextrins, sulfobutylether beta-cyclodextrin, sodium stearyl fumarate, fumaric acid, alginic acid, sodium alginate, propylene glycol alginate, citric acid, succinic acid, carbomer, docusate sodium, glycerylbehenate, glyceryl stearate, meglumine, arginine, polyethylene oxide, polyvinyl acetate phthalates and the like. 9. A process for the preparation of amorphous solid dispersion of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3- (phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 and at least one pharmaceutically acceptable excipient, comprising of: a) Dissolving the (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 and at least one excipient in a suitable solvent or mixture of solvents at a suitable temperature, b) removing the solvent from the reaction mixture to provide amorphous solid dispersion of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 and an excipient. 10. The process as claimed in claim-9, wherein, in step-a) the suitable excipient is selected from polyvinylpyrrolidone (povidone or PVP), polyvinylpolypyrrolidone, polysorbate, cross linked polyvinyl pyrrolidone (crospovidone), polyethylene glycol (macrogol or PEG), polyvinyl alcohol, polyvinyl chloride, polyvinyl acetate, propylene glycol, cellulose, cellulose acetate phthalate (CAP), methyl cellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC, its sodium and calcium salts), carboxymethylethyl cellulose (CMEC), ethyl cellulose, hydroxymethyl cellulose, ethylhydroxyethyl cellulose, hydroxyethylcellulose, hydroxypropyl cellulose (HPC), hydroxypropyl cellulose acetate 

 succinate (HPCAS), hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose (hypromellose or HPMC), hydroxypropyl methylcellulose acetate succinate (HPMC-AS), hydroxyethyl methyl cellulose succinate (HEMCS), hydroxypropyl methylcellulose phthalate (HPMC-P), hydroxypropyl methylcellulose acetate phthalate, microcrystalline cellulose (MCC), same as defined in the third aspect of the present invention; the suitable solvent is selected from chloro solvents, ketone solvents, C 1 -C 6 alcohol solvents, ester solvents, nitrile solvents, ether solvents or their mixtures and the suitable temperature ranges from 0Â°C to reflux temperature of the solvent used; wherein the ratio of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3- (phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 and excipient in the solid dispersion is about 1 :0.05 to about 1 :5. 11. A process for the preparation of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2- enamide compound of formula- 1, comprising of the following steps: a) Reacting 3-nitrobenzaldehyde compound of formula-2 with methyl 2-(dimethoxy phosphoryl)acetate compound of formula-3 in presence of a suitable base in a suitable solvent to provide methyl (2E)-3-(3-nitrophenyl)acrylate compound of formula-4, b) reducing the compound of formula-4 with a suitable reducing agent in a suitable solvent to provide methyl (2E)-3-(3-aminophenyl)acrylate compound of formula-5, c) reacting the compound of formula-5 with aqueous sodium nitrite and hydrochloric acid to provide diazo compound, followed by in-situ reacting the obtained diazo compound with thionyl chloride in presence of copper chloride in water to provide methyl (2E)-3- (3-(chlorosulfonyl)phenyl)acrylate compound of formula- 6, d) reacting the compound of formula-6 in-situ with aniline in presence of a suitable base in a suitable solvent to provide methyl (2E) 3-(3-(N-phenylsulfamoyl) phenyl) acrylate compound of formula-7, e) optionally purifying the compound of formula-7 using a suitable solvent, f) reacting compound of formula-7 with hydro xyl amine in presence of a suitable base in a suitable solvent to provide (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl) phenyl]prop-2- enamide compound of formula- 1 , 

 g) optionally purifying the compound of formula- 1 using a suitable solvent to provide (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1. 12. The process as claimed in claim- 11, wherein, in step-a), d), f) the suitable base is selected from organic or inorganic base; in step-b) the suitable reducing agent is selected from Fe, Fe in acidic media like NH 4 Cl or HC1 or acetic acid, Sn in acidic media like HC1, Zn dust, Zn in acidic media like HC1 or NH 4 Cl or acetic acid, stannous chloride (SnCI 2 ), NaBH 4 , LiAIH 4 , LiBH 4 , diborane, borane- THF complex, hydrazine hydrate, hydrogenation catalysts such as Pd/C, Pt/C, PtO 2 , Pd(OH)2, Raney nickel, Rhodium, sulfides, alkali metal dithionite, alkali metal dithionate and sodium amalgam; in step-a) to step-g) the suitable solvent is selected from alcohol solvents, ester solvents, chloro solvents, hydrocarbon solvents, ketone solvents, ether solvents, nitrile solvents, polar aprotic solvents and polar solvents like water and mixture thereof. 13. A process for the preparation of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2- enamide compound of formula- 1, comprising of the following steps: a) Reacting 3-nitrobenzaldehyde compound of formula-2 with methyl 2-(dimethoxy phosphoryl)acetate compound of formula-3 in presence of potassium carbonate in methanol to provide methyl (2E)-3-(3-nitrophenyl)acrylate compound of formula-4, b) reducing the compound of formula-4 with iron powder in presence of ammonium chloride in a mixture of ethyl acetate and water to provide methyl (2E)-3-(3-amino phenyl)acrylate compound of formula-5, c) reacting the compound of formula-5 with aqueous sodium nitrite and hydrochloric acid to provide diazo compound, followed by in-situ reacting the obtained diazo compound with thionyl chloride in presence of copper chloride in water to provide methyl (2E)-3- (3-(chlorosulfonyl)phenyl)acrylate compound of formula-6, d) reacting the compound of formula-6 in-situ with aniline in presence of sodium bicarbonate in a mixture of ethyl acetate and water to provide methyl (2E) 3-(3-(N- phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl)acrylate compound of formula-7, 

 e) purifying the compound of formula-7 using toluene and followed by methanol to provide pure compound of formula-7, f) reacting compound of formula-7 with hydroxyl amine in presence of sodium hydroxide in a mixture of methanol and water and followed by purifying in a mixture of tetrahydrofuran and isopropanol to provide (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl) phenyl] prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1, g) dissolving the compound of formula- 1 using a mixture of acetone and water and the adding water to the solution to provide (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl) phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1. 14. A process for the purification of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2- enamide compound of formula- 1, comprising of: a) Dissolving (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide in a suitable solvent selected from alcohol solvents, hydrocarbon solvents, ether solvents, ketone solvents, ester solvents, polar solvents like water or mixture thereof, b) optionally filtering the reaction mixture, c) adding water to the obtained filtrate in step-b), d) stirring the reaction mixture and filtering the precipitated solid to provide (2E)-N- hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula-1. 15. The process as claimed in claim- 14, wherein a process for the purification of (2E)-N- hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula-1, comprising of: a) Dissolving (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide in a mixture of acetone and water, b) filtering the reaction mixture, c) adding water to the obtained filtrate in step-b), d) stirring the reaction mixture and filtering the precipitated solid to provide (2E)-N- hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula-1. 16. (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 produced as claimed in claim 1-15 having acid impurity, methoxy impurity, amide impurity, Z-Isomer impurity, methyl ester impurity less than about 0.05% area by HPLC. 17. Use of amorphous (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 in the preparation of pharmaceutical composition. 18. (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula- 1 obtained according to any of the preceding claims having particle size distribution of D 90 less than 150 Î¼m, preferably less than 100 Î¼m; more preferably less than 50 Î¼Î¹Î·. 19. Use of amorphous solid dispersion of (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl] prop-2-enamide in the preparation of pharmaceutical composition. 20. (2E)-N-hydroxy-3-[3-(phenylsulfamoyl)phenyl]prop-2-enamide compound of formula-1 obtained according to the preceding claims having purity greater than 99.9%; preferably 99.95%; more preferably 99.98% as measured by HPLC.
